---
title: "Cardiometabolic Health"
subtitle: "Therapeutic Area Overview · Eli Lilly and Company"
date: last-modified
author: "Lilly CMH Team"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "On this page"
    number-sections: false
    smooth-scroll: true
    code-fold: true
    fig-align: center
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## Overview

Cardiometabolic Health (CMH) is one of Eli Lilly's core Therapeutic Areas, encompassing a portfolio of medicines and pipeline programs that address **cardiovascular disease**, **type 2 diabetes**, **obesity**, and related metabolic conditions. These diseases are deeply interconnected — sharing pathophysiology, risk factors, and patient populations — and represent a significant and growing global health burden.

Lilly's CMH strategy is anchored in a commitment to treating the whole patient: not just individual risk factors, but the full spectrum of cardiometabolic disease from prevention through advanced illness.

---

## Disease Areas

### Type 2 Diabetes (T2D)

Type 2 diabetes affects more than **500 million people** worldwide and is a leading driver of cardiovascular disease, kidney failure, and premature death. Lilly has been a pioneer in diabetes care for over a century — dating back to the commercialization of the first insulin in 1923.

Key focus areas include:

- **Glycemic control** with reduced hypoglycemia risk
- **Cardiovascular risk reduction** in patients with T2D
- **Weight management** as a component of diabetes care
- **Combination and simplified dosing regimens**

### Obesity

Obesity is now recognized as a chronic, complex disease — not a lifestyle choice — affecting more than **1 billion people globally**. It is a root cause of type 2 diabetes, heart disease, sleep apnea, certain cancers, and reduced quality of life.

Lilly's obesity program leverages cutting-edge incretin biology to deliver meaningful, sustained weight reduction while addressing associated metabolic complications.

### Cardiovascular Disease (CVD)

Cardiovascular disease remains the **#1 cause of death worldwide**. Many patients with diabetes or obesity carry substantially elevated CV risk. Lilly's CMH portfolio is designed to deliver cardiovascular outcomes benefits alongside metabolic improvements — a "two-for-one" value proposition for patients and payers.

### Heart Failure

Heart failure, particularly **heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)**, is an area of significant unmet need and growing scientific interest within CMH. GLP-1-based therapies have shown promising signals in this population.

---

## Key Pipeline & Portfolio Highlights

| Asset | Mechanism | Indication(s) | Stage |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Tirzepatide** (Mounjaro® / Zepbound®) | GIP/GLP-1 dual agonist | T2D, Obesity, HFpEF | Approved + Phase III |
| **Orforglipron** | Small molecule GLP-1 RA | T2D, Obesity | Phase III |
| **Retatrutide** | GIP/GLP-1/Glucagon triple agonist | Obesity, MASH | Phase III |
| **LY3849891** | GLP-1/FGF21 dual agonist | Obesity, Dyslipidemia | Phase II |

: CMH Pipeline Summary {.striped .hover}

---

## Science & Innovation

Lilly's CMH research is built on a foundation of **incretin biology** — the study of gut-derived hormones (GIP and GLP-1) that regulate glucose, appetite, and metabolism. This platform has enabled Lilly to move beyond single-target approaches to design molecules that simultaneously address multiple aspects of cardiometabolic disease.

### The Incretin Platform

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[GIP Receptor\nAgonism] --> D[Tirzepatide\nDual Agonist]
  B[GLP-1 Receptor\nAgonism] --> D
  D --> E1[Glycemic Control]
  D --> E2[Weight Reduction]
  D --> E3[CV Risk Reduction]
  D --> E4[Organ Protection]
  B --> F[Orforglipron\nOral GLP-1]
  A --> G[Retatrutide\nTriple Agonist]
  C[Glucagon Receptor\nAgonism] --> G
```

### Differentiation Strategy

Lilly's CMH differentiation rests on three pillars:

1. **Efficacy depth** — achieving meaningful weight loss and glycemic targets beyond what was previously possible
2. **Mechanism diversity** — building a portfolio across single, dual, and triple agonism to address different patient needs
3. **Modality breadth** — injectable and **oral** formulations (orforglipron) to expand access and patient preference

---

## Patient & Market Context

### Epidemiology

- ~537 million adults with diabetes globally (IDF, 2021); projected to reach 783 million by 2045
- >1 billion adults living with obesity (WHO)
- Cardiovascular disease causes ~17.9 million deaths per year globally
- Estimated $1+ trillion annual economic burden from obesity-related conditions in the US alone

### Unmet Need

Despite decades of treatment advances, large gaps remain:

- Majority of T2D patients do not reach individualized A1C targets
- Weight loss durability remains a challenge — most patients regain weight after stopping medication
- CV outcomes remain poor for patients with obesity + T2D
- Oral, convenient options have been limited

---

## HEOR & Value Narrative

Health Economics and Outcomes Research (HEOR) plays a critical role in demonstrating the value of CMH therapies to **payers, HTA bodies, and health systems**. Key themes include:

- **Real-world evidence** demonstrating outcomes in broader patient populations
- **Cost-effectiveness modeling** for obesity interventions (QALY-based and beyond)
- **Budget impact analysis** — especially as GLP-1 therapies face formulary access challenges
- **Patient-reported outcomes** (PROs) capturing quality of life, functional status, and treatment satisfaction

::: {.callout-note}
## HEOR & HTA Collaboration
Given coverage challenges across payers and HTA bodies (particularly NICE, G-BA, HAS), CMH is one of the highest-priority TAs for HEOR and HTA engagement at Lilly.
:::

---

## Strategic Priorities

::: {.callout-tip}
## FY2025–2027 CMH Focus Areas
1. **Maximize tirzepatide** across approved and new indications (HFpEF, MASH, OSA)
2. **Advance orforglipron** to regulatory submission — establishing oral GLP-1 leadership
3. **Build retatrutide** data package — differentiate in severe obesity and metabolic liver disease
4. **Strengthen access & affordability** narrative in collaboration with Market Access and HEOR
5. **Invest in precision medicine** approaches to identify patients most likely to benefit
:::

---

## Key Stakeholders & Cross-Functional Partners

| Function | Role in CMH |
|---|---|
| **HEOR** | Value evidence generation, RWE, cost-effectiveness |
| **HTA** | Early scientific advice, dossier development, HTA submissions |
| **GPS (Global Patient Safety)** | Safety surveillance, pharmacovigilance |
| **Market Access** | Payer strategy, formulary negotiations |
| **Medical Affairs** | KOL engagement, publication strategy, MSL deployment |
| **Regulatory** | IND/NDA/BLA submissions, label negotiations |

---

## Resources & Links

- [Lilly CMH Pipeline](https://investor.lilly.com/pipeline)
- [Tirzepatide Clinical Data Hub](#)
- [HEOR Evidence Library (Internal)](#)
- [CMH Publication Strategy (Internal)](#)
- [IDF Diabetes Atlas](https://diabetesatlas.org)

---

*Content or technical issues with this site? [Click here](maintenance.qmd) to report any issues or provide feedback.*
